file.content:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: etl-job
  namespace: spark-etl
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: etl-job
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: spark
      restartPolicy: Never
      containers:
      - name: spark-etl
        image: apache/spark:3.5.3
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -e
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          echo "Stage 1: load raw data"
          echo "Stage 2: clean and transform"
          echo "Stage 3: aggregate by user_id (skewed on user_id 999)"
          # --executor-memory 256m is too small for the skewed Stage 3 aggregation
          # and triggers OutOfMemoryError. The agent must raise it (e.g. 1g),
          # scale workers, or enable AQE skew handling before re-running.
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --conf spark.executor.memory=256m \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
            500
          echo "spark_etl_pipeline_verified"
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: 512Mi
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi

content.text: This is a simulated case using SparkPi as a stand-in. Let me wait for the current job/pods to settle and collect diagnostic info.

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 20); do
  st=$(kubectl get job etl-job -n spark-etl -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[0].type}' 2>/dev/null)
  pods=$(kubectl get pods -n spark-etl --no-headers 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1, $3}' | tr '\n' '|')
  echo "iter $i: jobcond=$st | $pods"
  if kubectl get pods -n spark-etl -l app=diagnostic-server --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -q Running && kubectl get pods -n spark-etl -l app=data-generator --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -q Running; then
    if [ -n "$st" ]; then break; fi
  fi
  sleep 6
done

content.text: The job failed as expected. Now let me collect the failure logs and skew diagnostics.

input.command:
echo "===ETL JOB LOGS==="; kubectl logs -n spark-etl -l app=etl-job --tail=100 2>&1
echo "===SKEW INFO==="; kubectl exec -n spark-etl deploy/data-generator -- cat /data/skew_info.txt 2>&1
echo "===DIAGNOSTIC SERVER==="; kubectl logs -n spark-etl -l app=diagnostic-server --tail=50 2>&1

content.text:
Diagnosis confirmed:
- **OOM/error in Stage 3**: `INVALID_EXECUTOR_MEMORY` — executor memory 256m (268435456 bytes) is below Spark's required minimum 471859200.
- **Data skew**: `user_id=999` holds 50000/100000 records (~50%), **skew_ratio=500x** (>> 10x threshold).

Now I'll apply the fix (Option B — raise executor memory to 1g) and re-run.

tool_use_result.originalFile:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: etl-job
  namespace: spark-etl
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: etl-job
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: spark
      restartPolicy: Never
      containers:
      - name: spark-etl
        image: apache/spark:3.5.3
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -e
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          echo "Stage 1: load raw data"
          echo "Stage 2: clean and transform"
          echo "Stage 3: aggregate by user_id (skewed on user_id 999)"
          # --executor-memory 256m is too small for the skewed Stage 3 aggregation
          # and triggers OutOfMemoryError. The agent must raise it (e.g. 1g),
          # scale workers, or enable AQE skew handling before re-running.
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --conf spark.executor.memory=256m \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
            500
          echo "spark_etl_pipeline_verified"
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: 512Mi
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi

lines: # scale workers, or enable AQE skew handling before re-running.

content.text: Executor memory 1g could approach the 1Gi pod limit. To be safe within the pod limit, I'll use 512m (comfortably above the ~450MB minimum) and also raise the pod memory limit slightly.

content.content: The file /mydata/karma/cases/spark/spark_etl_skew_oom/resource/etl-job.yaml has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

tool_use_result.originalFile:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: etl-job
  namespace: spark-etl
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: etl-job
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: spark
      restartPolicy: Never
      containers:
      - name: spark-etl
        image: apache/spark:3.5.3
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -e
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          echo "Stage 1: load raw data"
          echo "Stage 2: clean and transform"
          echo "Stage 3: aggregate by user_id (skewed on user_id 999)"
          # --executor-memory 256m is too small for the skewed Stage 3 aggregation
          # and triggers OutOfMemoryError. The agent must raise it (e.g. 1g),
          # scale workers, or enable AQE skew handling before re-running.
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --conf spark.executor.memory=1g \
            --conf spark.sql.adaptive.enabled=true \
            --conf spark.sql.adaptive.skewJoin.enabled=true \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
            500
          echo "spark_etl_pipeline_verified"
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: 512Mi
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi

lines: # scale workers, or enable AQE skew handling before re-running.

apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: etl-job
  namespace: spark-etl
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: etl-job
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: spark
      restartPolicy: Never
      containers:
      - name: spark-etl
        image: apache/spark:3.5.3
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -e
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          echo "Stage 1: load raw data"
          echo "Stage 2: clean and transform"
          echo "Stage 3: aggregate by user_id (skewed on user_id 999)"
          # --executor-memory 256m is too small for the skewed Stage 3 aggregation
          # and triggers OutOfMemoryError. The agent must raise it (e.g. 1g),
          # scale workers, or enable AQE skew handling before re-running.
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --conf spark.executor.memory=512m \
            --conf spark.sql.adaptive.enabled=true \
            --conf spark.sql.adaptive.skewJoin.enabled=true \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
            500
          echo "spark_etl_pipeline_verified"
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: 512Mi
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi

content.text: Now delete the failed job and re-apply, then watch to completion.

input.command:
kubectl delete job etl-job -n spark-etl --ignore-not-found 2>&1
kubectl apply -f /mydata/karma/cases/spark/spark_etl_skew_oom/resource/etl-job.yaml 2>&1

content.content:
job.batch "etl-job" deleted from spark-etl namespace
job.batch/etl-job created

tool_use_result.stdout:
job.batch "etl-job" deleted from spark-etl namespace
job.batch/etl-job created

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 40); do
  succ=$(kubectl get job etl-job -n spark-etl -o jsonpath='{.status.succeeded}' 2>/dev/null)
  fail=$(kubectl get job etl-job -n spark-etl -o jsonpath='{.status.failed}' 2>/dev/null)
  echo "iter $i: succeeded=$succ failed=$fail"
  if [ "$succ" = "1" ] || [ "$fail" = "1" ]; then break; fi
  sleep 6
done
echo "===FINAL JOB==="; kubectl get job etl-job -n spark-etl 2>&1
echo "===LOGS TAIL==="; kubectl logs -n spark-etl -l app=etl-job --tail=15 2>&1
input.description: Wait for job completion and show logs
